acceptance_targets: [CH14_CRAFT_001] Pass if a cold reader can answer from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:123-131 why the raw routing is visible now, why it was hidden in ordinary sessions, why Kai sees it before Petra's team, and what exact on-screen evidence tells him the transfers are his data being siphoned. Fail if the scene still depends mainly on an unattended open engineer shell handing him the archive reveal.

[CH14_DIALOGUE_001] Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:165 no longer mirrors the style-bible example's sequence of admission, exclusive-access justification, and cut-off self-defense, and if the revised line sounds recognizably Yusuf without obvious deja vu from the calibration sample. Fail if the confession still clearly tracks that exemplar's structure or phrasing.

[CH14_DIALOGUE_002] Pass if in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:179-197 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:221-229 no single line compresses Meridian's scheme or the chapter's moral into a polished slogan, and if at least one of Yusuf's explanations is visibly interrupted or self-corrected before completion. Fail if the exchange still contains aphoristic diagnosis, mini-speech explanation, or a clean mic-drop moral line where scene pressure should make the speech messier.

[CH14_EXAMPLE_LINE_LEAKAGE] Pass if the revised confession delivers the same emotional beat without reproducing the example line's four-part structure (yeah + admission + because-nobody-else + self-interruption). The revised passage should not contain 'nobody was going to have' or an equivalent phrase that mirrors the example line's justification logic in the same sentence position. Fail if the revised passage still follows the same structural template.
[RED-009] Ch14 track entry does not re-state rubber smell, ceiling height, or footfall acoustics.
[RED-014] Ch14 contains at most 2 glasses references. The 'took off / put on / rubbed / cleaned' cycle does not repeat more than once.
[RED-023] Ch14 'He'-opener rate is below 8% and 'His'-opener rate is below 6%.
[CON-002] Ch14's crash-elapsed-time reference is equal to or greater than Ch11's. The temporal distance does not shrink between chapters.
[length_chapter_14_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can answer from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:123-131 why the raw routing is visible now, why it was hidden in ordinary sessions, why Kai sees it before Petra's team, and what exact on-screen evidence tells him the transfers are his data being siphoned. Fail if the scene still depends mainly on an unattended open engineer shell handing him the archive reveal.
findings.problem: Kai's discovery of Yusuf's archive arrives as a plot handout. The post-incident audit conveniently leaves an engineer shell open, the incriminating transfer tab already expanded, and the anomaly legible enough for a non-engineer to decode at a glance. Because the chapter's turn depends on this reveal, the scene needs clearer access logic: why the routing is exposed only now, why Petra's team has not already seen it, and what specifically lets Kai know this is theft rather than audit traffic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the discovery setup in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:123-131. Keep Kai in the bay and keep the archive reveal, but add one concrete reason the emergency audit exposes raw routing only in this moment, one immediate obstacle or uncertainty before Kai reaches the conclusion, and one explicit reason Petra's team has not already processed or closed this view before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:137. The fix should make Kai earn the recognition through a specific label, timestamp pattern, or remembered calibration detail rather than through a conveniently open answer screen.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:165 no longer mirrors the style-bible example's sequence of admission, exclusive-access justification, and cut-off self-defense, and if the revised line sounds recognizably Yusuf without obvious deja vu from the calibration sample. Fail if the confession still clearly tracks that exemplar's structure or phrasing.
findings.problem: Yusuf's confession line is a near-verbatim echo of a calibration-only example line in the style bible: the same admission beat, the same exclusive-access rationale, and the same broken self-exculpation. Because this lands inside the chapter's central trust-break, it reads less like live speech and more like copied calibration material, which damages voice integrity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the confession turn at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:165 with fresh sentence architecture and diction specific to this scene's pressure. Keep Yusuf admitting the theft and trying, unsuccessfully, to justify it, but do not reuse the current scaffold of flat admission plus exclusive-access rationale plus broken disclaimer. Let the line carry more of Yusuf's stressed Birmingham/private register or technical redirection without reproducing the style-bible exemplar.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:179-197 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:221-229 no single line compresses Meridian's scheme or the chapter's moral into a polished slogan, and if at least one of Yusuf's explanations is visibly interrupted or self-corrected before completion. Fail if the exchange still contains aphoristic diagnosis, mini-speech explanation, or a clean mic-drop moral line where scene pressure should make the speech messier.
findings.problem: The Kai-Yusuf confrontation intermittently slips into composed diagnosis instead of pressured speech. Yusuf's corporate readout arrives as clean mini-speeches, and Kai answers with a polished moral summation. Those lines deliver the chapter's thesis too efficiently; they sound written-about rather than spoken-through at the moment a real argument should be jagged, partial, and interruptive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the confrontation across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:179-197 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:221-229. Keep the same facts on the table: code-structure similarity, server-authorized suppression, Kai's anger about Tomas, and Yusuf's inability to prove intent. But break the information into more pressured turn-taking: interruptions, self-corrections, unfinished technical phrasing, and accusations tied to concrete facts in the room instead of finished thesis lines.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised confession delivers the same emotional beat without reproducing the example line's four-part structure (yeah + admission + because-nobody-else + self-interruption). The revised passage should not contain 'nobody was going to have' or an equivalent phrase that mirrors the example line's justification logic in the same sentence position. Fail if the revised passage still follows the same structural template.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
2960: "So you stole from us."
2961: 
2962: "Yeah. I copied the files." The Birmingham was thicker in his voice now, the professional register failing. "I took them because nobody else was going to have them once that contract went through. That's not — I know that's not a defense."
2963:

findings.problem: Yusuf's confession -- 'Yeah. I copied the files. I took them because nobody else was going to have them once that contract went through. That's not -- I know that's not a defense' -- closely mirrors the structure and logic of his style-bible example line: 'Yeah. Yeah, I took the data. I took the data because if I didn't take the data, nobody was going to have it. That's not -- look, I know how that sounds.' The rhythm (yeah + admission + because-nobody-else + self-interruption + self-awareness), the justification logic ('nobody was going to have it'), and the trailing self-aware deflection ('that's not --') are near-verbatim. This occurs in a chapter-defining emotional beat where Yusuf's concealment is exposed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2960-2963 to deliver the same confession with a different sentence architecture. Yusuf's register should stay (Birmingham under stress, direct admission), but the progression from admission through justification to self-awareness needs a different shape. Option: Yusuf starts with the technical justification (what the data was for, how the access worked) and is pulled toward the personal admission by Kai's refusal to accept the technical frame. Option: Yusuf tries the professional defense first and it visibly fails before the raw version comes out, arriving in shorter sentences rather than the example line's cascading repetition.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 track entry does not re-state rubber smell, ceiling height, or footfall acoustics.
findings.evidence: Ch1 line 41: 'rubber surface followed by a fast, shallow echo… high ceilings with acoustic dampening'; Ch5 line 743: 'rubber smell… ceilings were high and packed with acoustic dampening'; Ch14 line 2802: 'indoor track smelled like hot rubber and recycled air… high ceilings swallowed the sound of their footfalls and returned it as faint percussion'
findings.problem: Third re-description of the indoor track's sensory inventory. By Ch14, the reader has been taught the rubber smell and the ceiling/footfall acoustics twice (Ch1, Ch5). Ch14 re-enters through the same elements again.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch14, cut the track's sensory inventory entirely. Enter through Tomas's POV — his counting rhythm, his body's relationship with the hurdles — which is already the chapter's strongest opening move at line 2800.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 contains at most 2 glasses references. The 'took off / put on / rubbed / cleaned' cycle does not repeat more than once.
findings.evidence: Ch14 contains 5 glasses references: line 2932 'glasses pushed up on his forehead'; line 2956 'took his glasses off his forehead and put them on'; line 3008 'took his glasses off again and rubbed his eyes'; line 3014 'put his glasses on, took them off again'; line 3036 inferred from scene. Manuscript total: 22 glasses references (target: 8-10 for 20+ images).
findings.problem: Yusuf's glasses gesture appears 5 times in Ch14 alone. Across the manuscript, the 'took off glasses / put them on / rubbed bridge of nose / cleaned on shirt' cycle appears 22 times. At 20+ instances, the target is 8-10 retained. Ch14's 5 instances (combined with Ch6's 5) represent nearly half the manuscript's total.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch14, keep 1-2 glasses references — the forehead-to-face adjustment at line 2956 (which marks the emotional shift to 'technical mode') is the strongest. Cut the remaining 3-4 instances. Yusuf's distress in this chapter should register through other channels.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 'He'-opener rate is below 8% and 'His'-opener rate is below 6%.
findings.evidence: Ch14: 33 of 338 sentences (9.8%) begin with 'He'; 28 (8.3%) begin with 'His'. Combined 'He'/'His' opener rate: 18.0%.
findings.problem: 'He' and 'His' together open 18% of sentences in Ch14, the manuscript's longest chapter and the site of the central catastrophe. The pronoun density blunts the chapter's impact.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch14, convert at least 10 'He'/'His'-opened sentences to alternative constructions, especially in the post-crash sequences where the pattern is densest.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch14's crash-elapsed-time reference is equal to or greater than Ch11's. The temporal distance does not shrink between chapters.
findings.evidence: Ch11 line 2013: 'Six weeks ago he'd been bleeding on the track'; Ch14 line 2802: 'his stride still favoring the shoulder that had healed wrong after the blackout crash five weeks ago'
findings.problem: The crash's temporal distance runs backward. Ch11 says the crash was six weeks ago; Ch14, which occurs later in the narrative, says it was five weeks ago. Time should move forward between chapters. Either Ch11 overstates the elapsed time or Ch14 understates it. Based on the continuity sheet (crash ~2 weeks after late-January arrival), Ch11 at roughly week 8 would be correct at 'six weeks since the crash,' making Ch14 at week 8-9 require 'six to seven weeks ago' — not 'five weeks.'
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch14 line 2802, change 'five weeks ago' to a figure consistent with Ch11's 'six weeks ago' — at minimum 'six weeks ago' or 'more than a month ago' or a phrasing that avoids a specific number if timeline flexibility is needed.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
